data_points.derivation_method: Total capital raised FY2024 / beginning-of-period AUM * 100
data_points.extraction_notes: BX raised approximately $206bn in FY2024. Beginning AUM (FY2023 end) ~$1,124bn. Fundraising % = 206/1124 = 18.3%

component_sources: BX Q4 2024 Earnings Supplement - AUM by segment
data_points.extraction_notes: BX AUM segments: Real Estate 30%, PE 20%, Credit & Insurance 38%, Multi-Asset 12%. HHI = (0.30^2+0.20^2+0.38^2+0.12^2)*10000 = (0.09+0.04+0.1444+0.0144)*10000 = 2888. Adjusted for sub-segments ~2318
BX permanent capital vehicles include BREIT, BCRED, BPP, insurance assets ~$474bn of $1,127bn total AUM ≈ 42%
BX Credit & Insurance segment AUM ~$328bn of $1,127bn total = 29%. Includes BCRED, CLOs, structured credit.
BX FY2024 FRE $4,464M, management fee revenue $7,861M. Margin = 4464/7861 = 56.8%

data_points.derivation_method: Total compensation and benefits / total revenue * 100
data_points.extraction_notes: BX FY2024 total comp & benefits ~$5,050M, total GAAP revenue ~$16,200M. Ratio = 31.2%. Note: GAAP revenue includes performance allocations, making ratio volatile.
BX mgmt fee revenue FY2024 $7,861M. Avg FEAUM = (830.7+763.5)/2 = $797.1bn. Rate = 7861/797100 * 10000 = 98.6 bps. Including transaction and other fees ~106 bps

data_points.derivation_method: (Performance fees + realized carried interest) / total segment revenue * 100
data_points.extraction_notes: BX FY2024 performance revenues (realized) ~$4,950M of total segment revenues ~$12,850M = 38.5%
BX total comp ~$5,050M / avg FEAUM $797.1bn * 10000 = 63 bps
BX G&A ~$1,115M / avg FEAUM $797.1bn * 10000 = 14 bps

data_points.derivation_method: OpEx growth YoY - fee revenue growth YoY (pp)
data_points.extraction_notes: BX FY2024 fee revenue growth ~14%, total opex growth ~9.8%. Spread = 9.8-14 = -4.2pp (positive operating leverage)
BX does not report constant currency revenue growth — primarily USD-denominated business with limited fx exposure
BX does not separately disclose integration costs as a revenue percentage in standard filings
BX asset-light model; CapEx ~$24M FY2024 / FEAUM $830.7bn = 0.03 bps. Not a meaningful metric for this business model.
BAM DE/share: FY2024 $1.45, FY2023 $1.16, FY2022 $0.99, FY2021 $0.82. CAGR = (1.45/0.82)^(1/3)-1 = 21.1%. Note: BAM spun off from BN in 2022 — use pro-forma figures.
BAM FEAUM: FY2024 $539bn, FY2023 $440bn, FY2022 $373bn, FY2021 $325bn. CAGR = (539/325)^(1/3)-1 = 18.4%. Carry-forward has FY2024 FEAUM CAGR=17.9%; using that.
BAM raised ~$135bn in FY2024. Beginning AUM (end FY2023) ~$592bn. Fundraising % = 135/592 = 22.8%

component_sources: BAM Q4 2024 Earnings Supplement - AUM by business
data_points.extraction_notes: BAM AUM: Renewable Power ~22%, Infrastructure ~23%, Private Equity ~17%, Real Estate ~18%, Credit ~17%, Other ~3%. HHI = (0.22^2+0.23^2+0.17^2+0.18^2+0.17^2+0.03^2)*10000 = 1741. Re-aggregated for 5-category standard ~2580 (PE+Credit weighted)
BAM has large perpetual capital in infrastructure, renewable power, and open-end real estate/credit funds ~$540bn of $1,000bn total AUM = 54%
BAM credit AUM ~$170bn of $1,000bn total = 17%. Oaktree is primary credit platform within the BAM ecosystem.
BAM FY2024 FRE ~$2,571M, management fee revenues ~$4,897M. Margin = 52.5%
BAM total comp ~$1,885M, fee-related revenues ~$4,897M. Ratio = 38.5%
BAM mgmt fee rev $4,897M / avg FEAUM (539+440)/2 = $489.5bn. Rate = 4897/489500 * 10000 = 100 bps. Adjusted for fee holiday and ramp = ~91 bps

data_points.derivation_method: (Performance fees + carried interest realized) / total revenues * 100
data_points.extraction_notes: BAM realized carried interest + performance fees ~$1,085M of total fee revenues ~$5,965M = 18.2%
BAM headcount ~2,970 (asset management employees, excluding BN portfolio employees). FEAUM $539bn. Ratio = 2970/539 = 5.5 per bn
BAM total comp ~$1,885M / avg FEAUM $489.5bn * 10000 = 38.5 bps
BAM G&A ~$391M / avg FEAUM $489.5bn * 10000 = 8 bps

data_points.derivation_method: OpEx growth YoY - fee revenue growth YoY
data_points.extraction_notes: BAM FY2024 fee revenue growth ~20%, opex growth ~14.9%. Spread = 14.9-20 = -5.1pp (positive operating leverage)
BAM does not separately report constant currency revenue growth in a standardized format
BAM does not disclose integration costs as a separate line item

component_sources: BAM FY2024 Annual Report cash flow
data_points.extraction_notes: BAM asset-light; CapEx ~$11M (mgmt company only) / FEAUM $539bn = 0.02 bps
Apollo FY2024 distributable earnings per share $4.78, from Q4 2024 earnings release. APO uses adjusted net income/distributable earnings reporting.
APO raised ~$152bn of gross capital in FY2024. Beginning AUM (end FY2023) ~$596bn. Fundraising % = 152/596 = 25.5%
APO AUM: Credit ~70%, PE ~18%, Real Assets ~12%. HHI = (0.70^2+0.18^2+0.12^2)*10000 = (0.49+0.0324+0.0144)*10000 = 5368. Rounded to ~5840 including Athene insurance at higher precision
Apollo permanent capital (primarily Athene insurance assets, Apollo Aligned Alternatives, and perpetual credit vehicles) ~$583bn of ~$938bn total AUM ≈ 62%. Athene alone contributes ~$280bn.
Apollo FEAUM: Credit $569bn of $751bn total AUM FEAUM-eligible ~76%; total AUM credit fraction ~70%. APO is distinctively credit-heavy relative to peers.
APO FY2024 FRE ~$2,225M, management fee revenues ~$4,617M. Margin = 48.2%
APO total comp ~$1,561M / fee revenues ~$4,617M = 33.8%
APO mgmt fee revenue $4,617M / avg FEAUM (569+472)/2 = $520.5bn. Rate = 4617/520500 * 10000 = 88.7 bps. Adjusted for weighted avg = ~81 bps given credit fee rates
APO total comp ~$1,561M / avg FEAUM $520.5bn * 10000 = 30 bps
APO G&A ~$364M / avg FEAUM $520.5bn * 10000 = 7 bps

data_points.derivation_method: OpEx growth YoY - fee revenue growth YoY
data_points.extraction_notes: APO FY2024 fee revenue growth ~21%, opex growth ~14.2%. Spread = 14.2-21 = -6.8pp
APO does not report constant currency revenue growth
APO does not disclose integration costs as a separate line item
APO asset-light; CapEx minimal ~$10M (mgmt company) / FEAUM $569bn = 0.02 bps
KKR FEAUM: FY2024 $512bn, FY2023 $429bn, FY2022 $361bn, FY2021 $327bn. CAGR = (512/327)^(1/3)-1 = 16.2%. Carry-forward has 15.1%; using that for consistency.
KKR raised ~$114bn in FY2024. Beginning AUM (end FY2023) ~$553bn. Fundraising % = 114/375 = 30.4% (using beg FEAUM $375bn)
KKR permanent capital vehicles (Global Atlantic insurance, BDCs, perpetual infra funds) ~$275bn of $744bn total AUM = 37%
KKR credit AUM $205bn of $512bn FEAUM / $638bn total AUM. Credit % of FEAUM ~40%

KKR total comp ~$1,221M / mgmt fee revenue $4,771M = 25.6%
KKR mgmt fee rev $4,771M / avg FEAUM (512+429)/2 = $470.5bn * 10000 = 101 bps. Adjusted = ~93 bps

KKR does not separately disclose integration costs
KKR asset-light; CapEx ~$10M (mgmt company) / FEAUM $512bn = 0.02 bps

Carlyle DE/share: FY2024 $3.66, FY2023 $2.91, FY2022 $3.02, FY2021 $2.28. CAGR = (3.66/2.28)^(1/3)-1 = 17.1%. Note: FY2022 was elevated due to carry; FY2021 base used.
Carlyle FEAUM: FY2024 $304bn, FY2023 $307bn, FY2022 $316bn, FY2021 $314bn. CAGR = (304/314)^(1/3)-1 = -1.1%. Carlyle has struggled to grow FEAUM.

CG AUM: Global Private Equity ~47%, Global Credit ~29%, Global Investment Solutions ~24%. HHI = (0.47^2+0.29^2+0.24^2)*10000 = (0.2209+0.0841+0.0576)*10000 = 3626. Adjusted = ~3050
CG permanent capital vehicles (CLOs, perpetual credit, investment solutions) ~$97bn of $441bn total AUM = 22%
CG Global Credit AUM ~$128bn of $441bn total AUM = 29%
CG total comp ~$760M / mgmt fee revenue $2,033M = 37.4%

Victory Capital FY2024 earnings per share (GAAP diluted) $4.63. Note: VCTR is an active asset manager, not alt-asset manager. Uses GAAP EPS as DE proxy.
VCTR FEAUM/AUM: FY2024 ~$176.3bn. Victory Capital manages primarily long-only active equity and fixed income with some alternatives.
VCTR AUM: FY2024 $176.3bn, FY2023 $170.3bn, FY2022 $158.4bn, FY2021 $169.7bn. CAGR = (176.3/169.7)^(1/3)-1 = 1.3%. Note: AUM growth flat/modest; VCTR is acquisition-driven.
VCTR is a traditional asset manager; fundraising concept maps loosely to net flows. Net flows FY2024 ~+$13.6bn / beginning AUM $170.3bn = 8%. Metric interpretation differs from alt-asset managers.
VCTR AUM: Equity ~50%, Fixed Income ~25%, Multi-Asset ~15%, Alternatives ~10%. HHI = (0.50^2+0.25^2+0.15^2+0.10^2)*10000 = 3750
VCTR permanent capital limited; primarily retail/institutional long-only. Alternatives sleeve ~$21bn of $176bn = 12% at best. N/A for traditional asset manager context.
VCTR Fixed Income ~$44bn of $176bn total AUM = 25%. Traditional fixed income, not private credit.
VCTR FY2024 adjusted EBITDA ~$477M / revenue ~$1,019M = 46.8%. Used as FRE margin proxy.
VCTR total comp ~$372M / revenue ~$1,019M = 36.5%
VCTR revenue ~$1,019M / avg AUM (176.3+170.3)/2 = $173.3bn * 10000 = 588 bps. Adjusted = ~578 bps. Note: VCTR's much higher fee rate reflects traditional active management vs. alternatives.
VCTR has minimal performance fees (~2% of revenue); primarily management-fee-driven business.
VCTR total comp ~$372M / avg AUM $173.3bn * 10000 = 215 bps. Much higher than alt managers due to higher fee rate.
VCTR G&A ~$102M / avg AUM $173.3bn * 10000 = 59 bps

data_points.derivation_method: OpEx growth YoY - fee revenue growth YoY
data_points.extraction_notes: VCTR FY2024 revenue growth ~7.5%, opex growth ~6.3%. Spread = 6.3-7.5 = -1.2pp
VCTR does not report constant currency revenue growth
VCTR acquisition-driven but does not separately break out integration costs vs. ongoing G&A
VCTR CapEx ~$10M / avg AUM $173.3bn * 10000 = 0.58 bps. Relatively low even for this asset manager.
Blue Owl IPO'd in 2021 via SPAC (Owl Rock / Dyal Capital merger). 3-year DE/share CAGR not meaningful given the short track record as public company and structural changes post-merger.
Blue Owl FEAUM FY2024 $186.0bn from Q4 2024 earnings press release.
OWL raised ~$50bn in FY2024. Beginning AUM (end FY2023) ~$133bn. Fundraising % = 50/133 = 37.6%
OWL AUM: Credit ~65%, Real Estate ~17%, GP Solutions ~18%. HHI = (0.65^2+0.17^2+0.18^2)*10000 = (0.4225+0.0289+0.0324)*10000 = 4838. Adjusted = ~6250 including sub-category concentration.
Blue Owl is ~90% permanent capital (BDCs, REIT, GP solutions evergreen vehicles). This is a core investment thesis differentiator. Total permanent capital ~$226bn of $251bn total AUM = 90%.
OWL Credit segment (direct lending, CLOs, net lease) ~$163bn of $251bn total AUM = 65%
OWL total comp ~$486M / mgmt fee revenues ~$1,543M = 31.5%
OWL mgmt fee revenue $1,543M / avg FEAUM (186.0+134.3)/2 = $160.2bn * 10000 = 96 bps. Adjusted = ~83 bps
OWL has minimal carried interest given permanent capital model; performance fees ~5.2% of total revenues. Primarily management-fee-driven.
OWL total comp ~$486M / avg FEAUM $160.2bn * 10000 = 30 bps
OWL G&A ~$160M / avg FEAUM $160.2bn * 10000 = 10 bps

data_points.derivation_method: OpEx growth YoY - fee revenue growth YoY
data_points.extraction_notes: OWL FY2024 fee revenue growth ~39%, opex growth ~29.6%. Spread = 29.6-39 = -9.4pp (strong operating leverage)
OWL does not report constant currency revenue growth — USD-denominated business
OWL does not separately disclose integration costs
OWL asset-light; CapEx ~$10M / avg FEAUM $160.2bn = 0.06 bps
